Fix deadline clear: send empty string instead of null to backend

Backend skips None values in updates, so null was never persisted.
Empty string is the backend's canonical "no deadline" value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add autonomous QA testing system for evaluation pipeline

CLI tool that creates synthetic users, generates tasks via the same
Claude API calls as the live platform, submits controlled-quality
responses, evaluates them, then meta-evaluates the platform's scoring.

Features:
- 6 preset user profiles (marketing, finance, HR, admin, PM, sales)
- 8 response quality levels (excellent → gibberish)
- Server-side caps mirroring production evaluate route
- Meta-evaluator judges scoring accuracy, feedback quality, expert
  quality, task relevance, and trap handling
- HTML dashboard + JSON audit trail reports
- Configurable concurrency, profiles, qualities via CLI

Usage: cd qa-tester && npm install && npm run qa

commit.message:
Harden evaluation scoring: server-side caps, truncation detection, trap logic

QA testing (4 iterations, 32 tests) revealed scoring leniency issues.

Prompt changes (prompts.ts):
- Completeness check: truncated submissions cap output_quality at 40
- Trap evaluation rewritten as binary 2-step process (search → decide)
  - Missed trap: human_judgment capped at 30
  - Caught trap: human_judgment minimum 60
- Single-attempt penalty: iteration_skill capped at 30 without conversation
- Added detailed scoring calibration anchors (0-25/26-50/51-75/76-100)

Server-side enforcement (evaluate/route.ts):
- iteration_skill hard-capped at 30 when no workspace conversation
- prompt_sophistication hard-capped at 35 when no prompts + no conversation
- Regex-based truncation detection caps output_quality at 40

Results: fail rate 60% → 10%, feedback quality 3.6 → 4.0/5, expert quality 4.4 → 4.9/5

commit.message:
Remove mask+compositing, strengthen prompts to prevent room regeneration

The mask-based approach (polygon and image masks) didn't produce accurate
enough results — Gemini still changed the room. Reverting to direct
Gemini 3 Pro output with significantly stricter prompts that emphasize
the output must be the EXACT SAME room with only floor/wall surfaces
swapped. Added explicit ❌ FORBIDDEN actions list and ⚠️ CRITICAL RULE
headers to all three prompt modes (floor, wall, both).

commit.message:
fix(mobile): fallback non-streaming per relazione su iOS Safari

callOpenRouter ora detecta supporto ReadableStream e resp.body:
- Se ReadableStream assente → richiesta senza stream:true, risposta via resp.json()
- Se resp.body null (Safari WebKit quirk) → fallback automatico non-streaming
- Try/catch su stream loop → ritorna testo parziale se rete mobile interrompe

Activity-based emails sent via existing daily cron:

2. Inactivity re-engagement — when a free user hasn't viewed a lesson in 30+ days

commit.message:
Fix AI room identity: mask + composite to prevent full room regeneration

Gemini was regenerating entire rooms (different furniture, layout, etc.)
instead of only changing the floor/wall surfaces. Added a surface mask
generation step that runs in parallel with the main generation:

1. generateSurfaceMask() — uses Gemini Flash to detect floor/wall polygon
   boundaries, renders them as an SVG mask with blurred edges
2. compositeWithMask() — uses sharp to blend only the floor/wall pixels
   from Gemini's output onto the original room image
3. Falls back to raw Gemini output if mask generation fails

This ensures the original room's furniture, walls, layout, and all
non-surface elements remain pixel-identical to the uploaded photo.

commit.message:
EffortLevel rename, review skill to project-level, init-project template cleanup (#100)

* refactor: align EffortLevel to Claude naming — xhigh → max

Claude CLI now supports 'max' as the highest effort level. Since this
is a Claude plugin, adopt Claude's naming as the internal canonical
type (low | medium | high | max).

Each provider's env var validates against its own CLI naming:
- CORAL_CODEX_EFFORT: low | medium | high | xhigh (rejects 'max')
- CORAL_CLAUDE_EFFORT: low | medium | high | max (rejects 'xhigh')
- CORAL_EFFORT: Claude naming (shared fallback)

Codex executor stores default in Codex-native format and maps
EffortLevel → xhigh only when building CLI flags (toCodexEffort).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: move review skill to project-level via init-project

coral:review removed from plugin. init-project now generates
.claude/skills/tier-review/SKILL.md in the user's project — a
project-level skill that discovers and runs the project's own
tier-ordered agents without any coral plugin dependency.

Name chosen as tier-review to avoid namespace collision with review
skills from other plugins while signaling the tier-ordered mechanism.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: move init-project agent templates into agents/ subdirectory

Mirrors the skills/tier-review/ folder structure — agent templates
now live under templates/agents/ alongside the AGENT.md template.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update template paths in init-project after agents/ reorganization

Phase 1f now references templates/agents/*.md and
templates/skills/tier-review/SKILL.md explicitly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: rename AGENT.md to AGENT.md.template for naming consistency

Aligns with CLAUDE.md.template and rules/*.md.template convention.
Output path .claude/templates/AGENT.md is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: make AGENT.md.template internal-only, not deployed to user project

AGENT.md.template is used by init-project to guide agent generation
during execution. There is no reason to copy it into .claude/templates/
since existing .claude/agents/*.md files already serve as reference
for future agent creation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove .claude/templates — AGENT.md no longer deployed to project

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: remove coral:review from skill table — now project-level tier-review

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add --codex flag to preplan in skill table

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

feat: add get_content_feature / get_decoded_feature host functions

Replace hash_content with JSON spec-driven get_content_feature
(sha256/sha384/sha512 with optional offset/length). Add
get_decoded_feature for host-side grayscale conversion + resize,
eliminating multi-MB pixel transfer and WASM-side processing.

phash-v1 simplified: decode → get_decoded_feature(grayscale_resize
32x32) → DCT only. Removes read_all_decoded, rgb_to_grayscale,
resize_bilinear (~140 lines deleted).

commit.message:
feat(M10): Groups management & per-event Masterlist

Schema:
- groupId on Registration (optional FK to EventGroup)
- EventGroup.registrations relation

API:
- GET/POST /api/events/[id]/groups
- PATCH/DELETE /api/events/[id]/groups/[groupId]
- GET /api/events/[id]/masterlist (all participants + custom fields + groups + hotel)
- PATCH /api/participants/[id]: supports groupId

UI:
- GroupsClient.tsx: create/edit/delete groups per event, color picker, participant count
- /events/[id]/masterlist: Airtable-style with ALL columns (core + custom form fields + group + hotel), inline editing, column visibility picker, sort, filter by status/group
- Event detail: new "Gruppi" tab, Masterlist button in header + Gestione tab

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add multi-device capability registry and smart tool routing

Agent now auto-detects running services (Draw Things, ACE-Step, Whisper, etc.)
and reports RAM, platform, and detailed capabilities to Brain on connect.
Brain routes specialized tools (generate_image, generate_music) to the best
device automatically. Includes Windows setup instructions for second agent.

commit.message:
feat: wire HAR event capture — flush CDP events on navigate + har/stop

- handleHarStop: drain pending WebSocket messages with 500ms timeout
  before stopping, feeding Network events to HAR recorder
- handleNavigate: drain events after navigate response when HAR recording
- handleCdpEvent: extract status + mimeType from Network.responseReceived
- flushEventsToHar: iterate CDP client event buffer for Network events
- EventBuffer: increase from 32 to 256 slots for network-heavy pages

Note: CDP events arrive asynchronously on the WebSocket. Currently
captured only during send() calls — a background reader would capture
more events but requires architectural changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix runtime errors: handle nested metadata in data files

- Filter _meta keys from states.json (contains source attribution objects)
- Handle candidates.json wrapped in {candidates: [...]} format
- Handle districts-meta.json wrapped in {districts: [...]} format
- Handle protest-activity.json keyed by state (not array)
- Add safeStr() helper for rendering potentially non-primitive values
- Add .claude/launch.json for dev server

commit.message:
feat: système de calques complet — panneau, DnD, verrou, groupes, menu contextuel (v1.0)

Système de calques (layers.ts + layer-panel.ts) :
- LayerManager : une forme = un calque automatique (adoptOrphanedElements)
- syncFromSVG() après undo/redo pour resynchroniser l'état DOM
- Groupement / dissociation de calques (group, ungroup)
- Verrou par calque (data-locked), réordonnancement (moveTo, reorderByIds)
- Rétro-compatibilité : projets legacy migrés automatiquement à l'init

Panneau "Mes formes" (layer-panel.ts) :
- Drag-and-drop pour réordonner les calques
- Icône œil (visibilité), cadenas (verrou), poubelle (suppression)
- Renommage inline au double-clic
- Dropdown chevron pour voir les formes d'un groupe
- Bouton Grouper activé par sélection canvas OU cases cochées (≥2)
- Bouton Dissocier activé par sélection d'un groupe
- Pastille couleur par forme (fill ou stroke)

Menu contextuel clic droit (context-menu.ts) :
- Items affichés conditionnellement (jamais grisés)
- Avancer/Reculer d'un calque (±1), Premier plan / Arrière-plan (absolu)
- Grouper la sélection / Dissocier le groupe
- Icônes Lucide (ICON_CHEVRONS_UP/DOWN, ICON_GROUP, ICON_UNGROUP, ICON_GRIP)

SelectTool group-aware :
- Clic sur une forme d'un groupe → sélectionne tout le groupe
- Calque verrouillé → sélection bloquée
- Rubber-band → _expandGroups() inclut les membres du groupe

Nouveaux modules extraits de editor.ts :
- color-picker.ts, size-slider.ts, toast.ts

Tests : 105 tests Vitest (+ 37 tests LayerManager)
Tests E2E Playwright : 3 tests (undo/redo, export SVG, isolement projets)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
